links.title: Protect Turkish Street Dogs
links.mailtoSubject: Yasadışı sokak köpekleri ve kedilerinin öldürülmesini durdurun/Stop illegal street-dog and cat killings
links.description:
On July 30th, 2024, Turkish lawmakers passed a law that mandated that stray dogs be removed from the streets.
Thousands of dogs are being poisoned and thrown into mass graves, run over by cars, beaten to death in the streets, and dumped on the edge of the mountain without food or shelter.
The ones that end up in overcrowded shelters will either be euthanized or starve to death due to a complete disregard for their care.

TNVR programs work in reducing stray populations and in eliminating the spread of rabies, but it's cheaper to kill them. Proving that money is more important than life in Turkey.

Turkey's tourism industry is projected to earn $64 billion in 2025.
Boycott Turkey and hit them where it hurts them the most, their wallet. Stop purchasing Turkish goods, and cancel your vacations for somewhere more humane, like Spain or Portugal. 

#Turkey #dogsofinstagram #doglovers

links.mailtoBody:
İlgili makamlara,

Bu mektup, Türkiye genelinde sokak köpek ve kedilerinin yasadışı olarak yakalanması, istismar edilmesi ve öldürülmesine ilişkin yaygın ve belgelenmiş raporlarla ilgili resmi bir şikayet mektubudur.

Bu, kamera önünde açıkça gerçekleştirilen bir zulümdür ve şu anda ulusal bir hesap verebilirlik ve itibar krizine dönüşmüştür.

Raporlar ve görüntüler tekrar tekrar şunları göstermektedir:

* kamuya açık alanlarda zehirlenme ve toplu ölümler
* toplama sırasında köpek ve kedilerin dövülmesi, vurulması veya şiddet uygulanması
* yasal veterinerlik gerekçesi olmaksızın hayvanların öldürülmesi
* şeffaf kayıtlar tutulmaksızın cesetlerin kaldırılması ve imha edilmesi
* bağımsız erişim veya kamu denetimi olmaksızın faaliyet gösteren barınaklar

Türkiye'nin hayvan koruma çerçevesi, “kamu güvenliği” bahanesiyle yasadışı öldürme, zulüm veya hesap verilmeyen imhaya izin vermemektedir. Ötenazi uygulanacaksa, bu yasal, kanıta dayalı ve veterinerlik otoritesi altında yapılmalı, belediyeler veya yükleniciler için bir kestirme yol olmamalıdır.

TALEPLERİMİZ (ACİL, GÖRÜNÜR EYLEM)

1. Tüm belediyeler ve yükleniciler aşağıdakileri derhal durdurmalıdır:

* zehirleme (yemleme dahil)
* toplama sırasında hayvanları vurma, dövme veya yaralama
* belgelenmiş veteriner kararı olmadan öldürme
* kayıtsız ölümler ve imha

2. Ulusal makamlar, suçlanan belediyeler ve yükleniciler hakkında soruşturma açmalı ve kanıtların desteklediği durumlarda cezai ve idari yaptırımlar uygulamalıdır.
   “Dahili olarak ele alınmak” hesap verebilirlik değildir. Bu, örtbas etmektir.

3. Kamuya ait hayvanları barındıran tesisler aşağıdakilere izin vermelidir:

* bağımsız denetim
* doğrulanmış STK erişimi
* hayvan başına denetlenebilir gözetim zinciri

KAMU GÜVENLİĞİ GERÇEĞİ: BİR TÜRÜ İSTİKRARSIZLAŞTIRMAK GÜVENLİK SAĞLAMAZ

Sokak hayvanlarının popülasyonları agresif bir şekilde ortadan kaldırıldıkça, sıçanlar, yılanlar, yaban domuzları ve kurtların kentsel alanlara daha yakın yerlerde görülmeye başladığı yaygın olarak gözlemlenmiştir. Tek bir neden öne sürülmese bile, ilke basittir: Bir ortamdan uzun süredir var olan türleri ortadan kaldırdığınızda, gelişmiş bir ekosistemi bozarsınız ve istenmeyen sonuçlar yaratırsınız.

* Sıçanlar hastalık taşır ve yiyecek atıkları bulunan ve avcıların baskısının azaldığı yerlerde çoğalır.
* Yılanlar avlarının bulunduğu yerleri takip ederler ve Türkiye'de zehirli yerli türler vardır.
* Yaban domuzları ve kurtlar “canavar” değildir, ancak kışkırtıldıklarında, tehdit edildiklerinde veya köşeye sıkıştırıldıklarında kendilerini savunabilirler ve savunurlar.

Köpekleri ve kedileri ortadan kaldırmak riski ortadan kaldırmaz, halkın maruz kaldığı şeyi değiştirir. Diğer hayvanların dolduracağı bir boşluk bırakır ve istikrarsızlık ve yetersiz atık kontrolü nedeniyle tehlikeyi artırır.

ÇÖZÜM TNVR'DIR, ZULÜM DEĞİL

Sıkı ve doğru bir şekilde uygulanan TNVR programı (yakalama, kısırlaştırma, aşılama ve geri bırakma), zamanla üremeyi azaltan, aşılama kapsamı sayesinde kuduz riskini azaltan ve hayvan sayısının doğal olarak azalmasıyla ekosistemin kademeli olarak uyum sağlamasına olanak tanıyan tek yaklaşımdır.

Şiddet içeren toplu uzaklaştırma ve “temizlik” operasyonları, kargaşa, panik, yerinden edilme ve tekrarlanan acı döngüleri yaratır. Bu da halkı daha güvende değil, daha güvensiz hale getirir.

ŞU AN BEKLEDİKLERİMİZ

* Köpek ve kedilerin yasadışı olarak öldürülmesinin derhal durdurulduğunu teyit eden bir kamuoyu açıklaması
* Yayınlanmış bir ulusal uygulama planı ve denetim çerçevesi
* Yayınlanmış belediye sonuç raporları (sayılar, yükleniciler, gözaltında ölümler)
* Videoda zulüm yaparken yakalanan herkes için gerçek sonuçlar

Yanıt vermeme, reddetme olarak değerlendirilecektir. Sessizlik, onay olarak değerlendirilecektir.

Saygılarımızla,

@HallieForAnimals Ekibi
contact@hallieforanimals.org

To whom it may concern,

This is a formal escalation regarding widespread, documented reports of street dogs and cats being unlawfully captured, abused, and killed across Türkiye.

This is cruelty on camera, carried out openly, and it is now a national accountability and reputational crisis.

Reports and footage repeatedly show:

* poisoning and mass die-offs in public areas
* dogs and cats beaten, shot, or violently handled during collection
* animals killed without lawful veterinary justification
* bodies removed and disposed of without transparent records
* shelters operating without independent access or public oversight

Türkiye’s animal protection framework does not permit illegal killing, cruelty, or unaccountable disposal under the cover of “public safety.” If euthanasia occurs, it must be lawful, evidence-based, and under veterinary authority not a shortcut for municipalities or contractors.

WHAT WE ARE DEMANDING (IMMEDIATE, VISIBLE ACTION)

1. All municipalities and contractors must immediately cease:

* poisoning (including baiting)
* shooting, beating, or injuring animals during collection
* killing without documented veterinary decision-making
* unrecorded deaths and disposal

2. National authorities must open investigations into implicated municipalities and contractors and pursue criminal and administrative penalties where evidence supports it.
   “Handled internally” is not accountability. It is concealment.

3. Facilities holding public animals must allow:

* independent inspection
* verified NGO access
* auditable chain-of-custody per animal

PUBLIC SAFETY REALITY: DESTABILISING ONE SPECIES DOES NOT CREATE SAFETY

It has been widely noted that rats, snakes, boar, and wolves are appearing closer to urban areas as street animal populations are aggressively removed. Even without claiming a single cause, the principle is simple: when you remove long-established species from an environment, you disrupt a developed ecosystem and create unintended consequences.

* Rats carry disease and thrive where food waste is available and pressure from predators decreases.
* Snakes follow prey availability, and Türkiye has venomous native species.
* Wild boar and wolves are not “monsters,” but they can and will defend themselves if provoked, threatened, or cornered.

Removing dogs and cats does not eliminate risk — it changes what the public is exposed to. It leaves a vacuum that other animals will fill, and it increases danger through instability and poor waste control.

THE SOLUTION IS TNVR NOT CRUELTY

A strict, properly enforced TNVR program (trap neuter vaccinate return) is the only approach that reduces breeding over time, reduces rabies risk through vaccination coverage, and allows the ecosystem to adapt gradually as animal numbers naturally decline.

Violent mass removals and “clean-ups” create churn, panic, displacement, and repeated cycles of suffering. while leaving the public less safe, not more safe.

WHAT WE EXPECT NOW

* A public statement confirming an immediate halt to illegal killings of dogs and cats
* A published national enforcement plan and inspection framework
* Published municipal outcome reporting (numbers, contractors, deaths in custody)
* Real consequences for anyone caught on video committing cruelty

Non-response will be treated as refusal. Silence will be treated as consent.

Sincerely,

@HallieForAnimals Team
contact@hallieforanimals.org

links.title: Cop Hit and Run, Arica, Chile
links.mailtoSubject: Investigar el flagrante desprecio por la compasión policial/Investigate flagrant disregard of police compassion
links.description:
PLEASE SEND THE ONE-CLICK EMAIL IN MY LINKTREE TO ARICA AUTHORITIES TITLED "COP HIT AND RUN, ARICA, CHILE"❗️

Call:
Carabineros de Chile: +56 582 458 078
Fiscalía de Arica: +56 600 333 0000
Municipalidad de Arica: +56 432 380 979

Location: Arica, Chile

A police vehicle intentionally runs over a small dog (Blanquito) and drives away.
Blanquito is receiving treatment for his injuries.

#AricayParinacotaRegion #Arica #cutedogs

links.mailtoBody:
A quien corresponda:

Les escribimos para llamar urgentemente la atención sobre un incidente profundamente preocupante ocurrido en Arica, Chile, donde un perro indefenso llamado Blanquito fue atropellado por un vehículo de los Carabineros y abandonado sin recibir asistencia. Este acto de negligencia, captado por las cámaras de seguridad, muestra cómo el vehículo no se detuvo a pesar de las súplicas desesperadas de los transeúntes. Blanquito, que no representaba ninguna amenaza y simplemente se encontraba fuera de su casa, sufre ahora lesiones graves y está hospitalizado, luchando por su vida.

Este incidente no solo constituye una violación de la compasión básica, sino también un claro incumplimiento de la responsabilidad encomendada a las fuerzas del orden de proteger y servir a todos los miembros de la comunidad, incluidos los más vulnerables. La falta de asistencia y el desprecio por la indignación pública exigen una rendición de cuentas inmediata.

Le instamos a:

1. Iniciar una investigación exhaustiva y transparente sobre este incidente, garantizando que los responsables rindan cuentas.
2. Implementar protocolos más estrictos para la conducción de vehículos y la respuesta a incidentes relacionados con el bienestar animal en los que se vean implicados vehículos institucionales.
3. Abordar públicamente este caso, asegurando a la comunidad que no se tolerará tal negligencia y que se están tomando medidas para evitar que se repita en el futuro.

La historia de Blanquito ha tenido un gran impacto entre los defensores de los animales y los ciudadanos preocupados de todo el mundo. Nos mantenemos unidos para exigir justicia y un cambio sistémico que proteja a los animales de cualquier daño y garantice que quienes ocupan puestos de autoridad mantengan los más altos estándares de integridad y compasión.

Solicitamos una declaración pública inmediata en la que se describan las medidas que su oficina u organización tomará para abordar este asunto. El mundo está pendiente de ello, y no descansaremos hasta que se haga justicia.

Prueba en vídeo: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1L9UjinErACrcapyje5vAb_bLWAb5hCE4/view?usp=sharing

Atentamente,



El equipo de @HallieForAnimals
contact@hallieforanimals.org




To Whom It May Concern,

We are writing to bring urgent attention to a deeply troubling incident that occurred in Arica, Chile, where a defenseless dog named Blanquito was struck by a Carabineros vehicle and left abandoned without aid. This act of negligence, captured on CCTV, shows the vehicle failing to stop despite the desperate pleas of bystanders. Blanquito, who posed no threat and was simply outside his home, now suffers from severe injuries and is hospitalized, fighting for his life.

This incident is not only a violation of basic compassion but also a clear breach of the responsibility entrusted to law enforcement to protect and serve all members of the community—including our most vulnerable. The failure to render aid and the disregard for public outcry demand immediate accountability.

We urge you to:

1. Launch a thorough and transparent investigation into this incident, ensuring those responsible are held accountable.
2. Implement stricter protocols for vehicle operation and response to animal welfare incidents involving institutional vehicles.
3. Publicly address this case, reassuring the community that such negligence will not be tolerated and that steps are being taken to prevent future occurrences.

Blanquito’s story has resonated deeply with animal advocates and concerned citizens worldwide. We stand united in demanding justice and systemic change to protect animals from harm and ensure that those in positions of authority uphold the highest standards of integrity and compassion.

We request a prompt public statements outlining the actions your office or organization will take to address this matter. The world is watching, and we will not rest until justice is served.

Video evidence: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1L9UjinErACrcapyje5vAb_bLWAb5hCE4/view?usp=sharing

Sincerely,



@HallieForAnimals Team
contact@hallieforanimals.org
links.callScript:
I am calling to report and demand action regarding the case of Blanquito, the dog who was hit by a Carabineros vehicle in Arica and abandoned without receiving help. This incident was captured on video and has caused outrage in both the local and international community.
We are formally requesting that your office immediately launch a thorough investigation into this incident. The community expects accountability, transparency, and concrete measures to ensure this never happens again. We will be sending an email with the evidence and details for your records.
We urge you to prioritize this case and take swift action. Thank you for your attention, and we look forward to your prompt response and updates on the steps being taken.

links.title: Demand Full Sentencing For Kristine Hicks
links.mailtoSubject: Enforce the Full Extent of the Law – Kristine Michelle Hicks Must Face Maximum Penalties
links.description:
PLEASE SEND THE ONE-CLICK EMAIL IN MY LINKTREE TO FEDERAL PROSECUTORS & TEXAS AUTHORITIES TITLED "DEMAND FULL SENTENCING FOR KRISTINE HICKS"❗️

Call:
U.S. Attorney’s Office, Eastern District of Texas: +1 409 839 2538
Hopkins County Sheriff Lewis Tatum: +1 903 438 4040
District Attorney Will Ramsay: +1 903 885 0641

Location: Cumby, Texas

Remember Kristine Hicks?
She's now been federally indicted for:
1 count of acting as an animal dealer without a license
4 counts of wire fraud.
Alongside state charges of:
1 count of cruelty to a non-livestock animal.
1 count of possession of marijuana.

She's facing up to twenty years in federal prison. Let's make sure she stays there.

#Texas #Cumby #doglover

#Texas #Cumby #doglover
links.mailtoBody:
To whom it may concern,

The recent federal indictment of Kristine Michelle Hicks for unlicensed animal dealing, wire fraud, and animal cruelty presents a critical opportunity for Texas to send an unequivocal message: 
Animal abuse will not be tolerated, and those who commit such acts will face the full force of the law.

- Animal abuse is a proven predictor of violent crime. Research shows that 70% of animal abusers have committed other criminal offenses, and nearly 40% have committed violent crimes against people.
- The FBI tracks animal cruelty alongside felony crimes like arson, assault, and homicide, recognizing its role as a “predictor crime” for future violence.
- In households where animal abuse occurs, there is an 88% overlap with child abuse and a 70%+ overlap with domestic violence.
- Hicks’ actions—shooting a dog tied to a tree, operating an unlicensed breeding facility, and defrauding buyers—demonstrate a pattern of cruelty and deception that cannot go unpunished.

We Demand:

1. Maximum penalties for Kristine Michelle Hicks under federal and state law, reflecting the severity of her crimes and serving as a deterrent to others.
2. Public accountability to ensure transparency in the legal process and restore faith in our justice system.
3. Strengthened enforcement of animal welfare laws to prevent future abuses and protect vulnerable animals and families.

We urge you to take immediate action to ensure Hicks is held fully accountable. The eyes of Texas, the nation, and the world are watching.

Video evidence: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1TE4gpazpqyzQj1uEie__eeMR_X3EQ8lw/view?usp=sharing

Sincerely,



@HallieForAnimals Team
contact@hallieforanimals.org

P.S. For further reading on the link between animal abuse and violent crime, see the FBI’s Law Enforcement Bulletin: The Link Between Animal Cruelty and Human Violence: https://leb.fbi.gov/articles/featured-articles/the-link-between-animal-cruelty-and-human-violence
links.callScript:
I’m calling about the federal case against Kristine Michelle Hicks.
I respectfully ask that the office pursue the maximum penalties allowed under federal law.
This is a case of protecting citizens, as animal abusers are significantly more likely to commit child abuse, sexual assault, domestic violence, and even murder.
Thank you for your time.

links.title: Stop The Cat & Dog Meat Trade in Vietnam
links.mailtoSubject: Việt Nam phải chấm dứt hoạt động buôn bán thịt chó và mèo tàn bạo/Vietnam Must End the Brutal Dog and Cat Meat Trade
links.description:
PLEASE SEND THE ONE-CLICK EMAIL IN MY LINKTREE TO VIETNAMESE AUTHORITIES TITLED "STOP THE CAT & DOG MEAT TRADE IN VIETNAM"❗️

In Vietnam, an estimated 6 million dogs and 1 million cats are slaughtered for meat every year. Animals are stolen from the streets and from the homes of families, pets ripped from their owners' gardens, still wearing their collars. They're transported for hours or days in wire cages, without food or water, piled on top of one another. They're killed by electrocution, by bludgeoning, and by throat-cutting, often while fully conscious.
The dog and cat meat trade isn't a controlled industry; it operates in the open, in markets and restaurants, largely unchallenged by law. Despite growing opposition from younger Vietnamese generations and repeated calls from international animal welfare organisations, the Vietnamese government has failed to act. Hanoi and Ho Chi Minh City have issued non-binding requests to end the trade, but without legislation, they're meaningless.
The trade is a significant driver of rabies and cholera outbreaks, posing a direct public health risk to both Vietnamese citizens and the millions of tourists who visit each year. TNVR programs have proven effective in managing stray populations and controlling the spread of disease, but it's more profitable to steal, cage, and kill.
Vietnam's tourism industry generated over $23 billion in 2024 and is growing fast. Boycott Vietnam until its government acts. Stop purchasing Vietnamese goods, and redirect your travel money to countries that don't profit from the suffering of companion animals.

#animallover #Vietnam #doglover

links.mailtoBody:
Kính gửi quý vị,

Chúng tôi viết thư này với tư cách là những công dân toàn cầu, những người bảo vệ động vật và những cá nhân quan tâm, để yêu cầu cấm hoàn toàn và ngay lập tức hoạt động buôn bán thịt chó và mèo tại Việt Nam. Ngành công nghiệp tàn nhẫn và lạc hậu này không chỉ gây ra những đau khổ không thể tưởng tượng được cho hàng triệu con vật, mà còn đe dọa sức khỏe cộng đồng, làm tổn hại đến uy tín quốc tế của Việt Nam, và đi ngược lại các giá trị của một xã hội hiện đại, giàu lòng nhân ái.

- Hơn sáu triệu con chó và một triệu con mèo bị giết mổ dã man tại Việt Nam mỗi năm. Hầu hết những con vật này là thú cưng bị đánh cắp hoặc động vật trong cộng đồng, bị tách khỏi nhà và gia đình, chỉ để phải đối mặt với những điều kiện khủng khiếp, bị nhốt chật chội trong lồng, bị bỏ đói và phải chịu những phương pháp giết mổ tàn bạo.
- Công dân Việt Nam áp đảo phản đối ngành công nghiệp này. Một cuộc khảo sát năm 2021 của FOUR PAWS cho thấy 91% người Việt Nam cho rằng ngành công nghiệp này nên bị cấm hoặc hạn chế, và 95% khẳng định nó không phải là một phần của văn hóa Việt Nam. Các thành phố như Hà Nội đã bắt đầu thực hiện các bước để loại bỏ việc tiêu thụ thịt chó, chứng minh rằng sự thay đổi là có thể và được mong đợi.
- Hoạt động buôn bán này chủ yếu diễn ra ngoài vòng pháp luật. Mặc dù có các quy định hiện hành, việc thi hành luật yếu kém và tham nhũng đã cho phép ngành công nghiệp này tồn tại. Các mạng lưới tội phạm tiếp tục buôn lậu động vật qua các tỉnh, đánh cắp vật nuôi và trốn tránh trách nhiệm.

Việt Nam không thể tự xưng là một quốc gia “văn minh” và tiến bộ trong khi vẫn cho phép hoạt động buôn bán man rợ này tiếp diễn. Thế giới đang theo dõi khi các quốc gia như Hàn Quốc thực hiện các biện pháp quyết liệt để cấm ngành công nghiệp thịt chó. Việt Nam phải làm theo, không chỉ vì lợi ích của động vật mà còn vì sức khỏe, an toàn và nhân phẩm của người dân.

Chúng tôi yêu cầu:

1. Cấm hoàn toàn việc buôn bán thịt chó và mèo trên toàn quốc, kèm theo các hình phạt nghiêm khắc đối với những ai vi phạm.
2. Thực thi nghiêm ngặt các luật hiện hành, bao gồm các chiến dịch trấn áp buôn lậu, giết mổ và bán chó mèo làm thịt.
3. Hỗ trợ các chương trình chuyển đổi để giúp người lao động trong ngành này chuyển sang các nghề nghiệp nhân đạo và bền vững.
4. Các chiến dịch giáo dục cộng đồng nhằm nâng cao nhận thức về sự tàn ác của ngành này và những rủi ro mà nó gây ra cho sức khỏe cộng đồng.

Đã đến lúc hành động. Việt Nam phải xứng đáng với tiềm năng của mình như một quốc gia nhân ái và tiến bộ.


Thân ái,



Đội ngũ @HallieForAnimals
contact@hallieforanimals.org


To whom it may concern,

We write to you as global citizens, animal advocates, and concerned individuals to demand an immediate and total ban on Vietnam’s dog and cat meat trade. This cruel and outdated industry not only inflicts unimaginable suffering on millions of animals but also threatens public health, undermines Vietnam’s international reputation, and contradicts the values of a modern, compassionate society.

- Over six million dogs and one million cats are brutally slaughtered in Vietnam every year. Most of these animals are stolen pets or community animals, torn from their homes and families, only to face horrific conditions, cramped in cages, starved, and subjected to brutal slaughter methods.
- Vietnamese citizens overwhelmingly reject this trade. A 2021 survey by FOUR PAWS found that 91% of Vietnamese believe the trade should be banned or discouraged, and 95% say it is not part of Vietnamese culture. Cities like Hanoi are already taking steps to phase out dog meat consumption, proving that change is possible and desired.
- The trade operates largely outside the law. Despite existing regulations, enforcement is weak, and corruption allows the industry to persist. Criminal networks continue to traffic animals across provinces, stealing pets and evading accountability.

Vietnam cannot claim to be a “civilized” and forward-thinking nation while allowing this barbaric trade to continue. The world is watching as countries like South Korea take decisive action to ban the dog meat industry. Vietnam must follow suit, not just for the sake of animals but for the health, safety, and dignity of its people.

We demand:

1. A nationwide ban on the dog and cat meat trade, with strict penalties for those who violate it.
2. Strong enforcement of existing laws, including crackdowns on illegal trafficking, slaughter, and sale of dogs and cats for meat.
3. Support for transition programs to help workers in the trade move to humane and sustainable livelihoods.
4. Public education campaigns to raise awareness about the cruelty of the trade and the risks it poses to public health.

The time for action is now. Vietnam must live up to its potential as a compassionate and progressive nation.


Sincerely,



@HallieForAnimals Team
contact@hallieforanimals.org
links.caption:
PLEASE SEND THE ONE-CLICK EMAIL IN MY LINKTREE TO VIETNAMESE AUTHORITIES TITLED "STOP THE CAT & DOG MEAT TRADE IN VIETNAM"❗️

In Vietnam, an estimated 6 million dogs and 1 million cats are slaughtered for meat every year. Animals are stolen from the streets and from the homes of families, pets ripped from their owners' gardens, still wearing their collars. They're transported for hours or days in wire cages, without food or water, piled on top of one another. They're killed by electrocution, by bludgeoning, and by throat-cutting, often while fully conscious.
The dog and cat meat trade isn't a controlled industry; it operates in the open, in markets and restaurants, largely unchallenged by law. Despite growing opposition from younger Vietnamese generations and repeated calls from international animal welfare organisations, the Vietnamese government has failed to act. Hanoi and Ho Chi Minh City have issued non-binding requests to end the trade, but without legislation, they're meaningless.
The trade is a significant driver of rabies and cholera outbreaks, posing a direct public health risk to both Vietnamese citizens and the millions of tourists who visit each year. TNVR programs have proven effective in managing stray populations and controlling the spread of disease, but it's more profitable to steal, cage, and kill.
Vietnam's tourism industry generated over $23 billion in 2024 and is growing fast. Boycott Vietnam until its government acts. Stop purchasing Vietnamese goods, and redirect your travel money to countries that don't profit from the suffering of companion animals.

#animallover #Vietnam #doglover

links.title: Protect Abused & Neglected Dogs in LA
links.mailtoSubject: Demand for Immediate Accountability for Animal Welfare Enforcement Failures in Los Angeles County
links.description:
PLEASE SEND THE ONE-CLICK EMAIL IN MY LINKTREE TO LA COUNTY & CITY LEADERSHIP TITLED "PROTECT ABUSED & NEGLECTED DOGS IN LA"❗️

Call:
LA County Supervisor Solis: +1 213 974 4111
LA County Supervisor Mitchell: +1 213 974 2222
LA County Supervisor Horvath: +1 213 974 3333
LA County Supervisor Hahn: +1 213 974 4444
LA County Supervisor Barger: +1 213 974 5555
SEAACA: +1 562 803 3301
LA Animal Services: +1 213 482 9558
LA DACC: +1 562 940 6898

In LA County, dogs are left to die in wire crates under plastic tarps in the scorching midday heat, without food or water. They limp through trash-strewn streets with open wounds and protruding ribs, used as test subjects for fentanyl, and beaten to death with crowbars. Pregnant dogs give birth on the pavement without care. Animals are tied up, tangled in ropes, crammed into cages for days, and abandoned to suffer in the sun. These are documented cases, filmed and reported, repeatedly by frontline rescuers who have been sounding the alarm for years.
Yet when advocates call for help, they are passed between police and animal control, neither of whom acts. Mayor Karen Bass announced a task force to address the crisis on Skid Row in 2025. After a single high-profile bust, the program vanished, with officers admitting it was disbanded after just a few trainings. Meanwhile, the Mayor proposes cutting $6.4 million from LA Animal Services, a department already so overwhelmed that shelters are euthanizing animals en masse.
California Penal Code 597 already prohibits animal cruelty. The laws exist. The budgets are allocated. The salaries, $301,000 for the Mayor, over $1.2 million collectively for the County Board of Supervisors, are paid. What is missing is the will to enforce the law.
Los Angeles is a city that prides itself on progress, on compassion, on being a leader. But on its streets, animals are dying from neglect and abuse while those paid to protect them look the other way. The suffering is not a secret. It is filmed daily, reported by news outlets, and witnessed by thousands. The question is no longer if this is happening. It is why those in power allow it to continue.
Boycott Los Angeles until its leaders act. Do not visit, do not spend, do not fund a city that turns a blind eye to cruelty. Redirect your tourism, your events, and your money to places that enforce their own laws. The animals of LA deserve better. Demand it.


#California #animallover #LosAngeles

It is filmed daily, reported by news outlets, and witnessed by thousands. The question is no longer if this is happening. It is why those in power allow it to continue.
Boycott Los Angeles until its leaders act. Do not visit, do not spend, do not fund a city that turns a blind eye to cruelty. Redirect your tourism, your events, and your money to places that enforce their own laws. The animals of LA deserve better. Demand it.


#California #animallover #LosAngeles
links.mailtoBody:
To whom it may concern,

We are writing on behalf of thousands of concerned citizens, animal advocates, and taxpayers who are demanding immediate and meaningful accountability from the leadership of Los Angeles County and the City of Los Angeles regarding the ongoing crisis of animal abuse and neglect taking place in plain sight across the region.

On Skid Row, the situation has reached a level of cruelty that is no longer ignorable. Dogs are confined in wire crates sweltering beneath plastic tarps in the midday heat without food or water. Others limp through trash-strewn streets with open wounds and protruding ribs. Volunteers on the ground have documented dogs being used to test drugs for fentanyl lethality, with several animals dying from overdoses as a result. Pregnant dogs give birth on the streets without medical care. Animals are tied up, tangled in ropes, crammed into tiny cages for days, beaten, and left in the sun without shade. These documented cases have been filmed and reported repeatedly by frontline rescue organizations who have been sounding the alarm for years.

Beyond Skid Row, the crisis extends across the county. In San Fernando, a pet sitter allegedly strangled a 22-month-old American Bully named Chula while her owners were away. In La Verne, a man allegedly beat his family's elderly corgi-chihuahua mix, Hope, to death with a crowbar because her barking disturbed his sleep. In another case, a man allegedly dropped a bulldog named Adriana on her head, kicked her, and threw her into a stack of tires, where she was left to die. These are symptoms of a county in which the message, intentionally or not, is that cruelty carries no real consequences.

When advocates call the police, they are told to call animal control. When animal control is called, they are told to call the police. Calls to the Mayor's office, LA Animal Services, and the LAPD to report dogs being beaten, starved, neglected, and abandoned typically go unanswered. In November 2025, Mayor Karen Bass announced a dedicated task force to address animal cruelty on Skid Row. After a single highly-publicized bust, advocates report they never saw evidence of the task force again, with officers recorded stating the program was disbanded after just a couple of trainings.

Los Angeles rules dictate that the issuance of new breeding licences be suspended when animal shelters exceed 75% capacity, a threshold the city has been well over for years, with shelters currently running at more than double their intended capacity. Meanwhile, on Skid Row, dogs are openly bred and sold in plain sight, with enforcement virtually non-existent. Mayor Bass has proposed cutting millions from the already stretched LA Animal Services department and laying off 62 of its employees, cuts that, according to the department's own interim general manager, would force the closure of at least three of the city's six shelters, in a system that is already overcrowded, understaffed, and euthanizing animals at a historic rate.

Let us be clear about what the taxpayers of Los Angeles are funding. Mayor Karen Bass draws an annual salary of approximately $301,000. Each of the five members of the LA County Board of Supervisors earns $244,727 per year, a collective salary bill for that body alone of over $1.2 million annually. These are the people elected and paid to govern this city and county. For that level of public investment, the very least Angelenos, and indeed a watching world, should expect is the basic enforcement of laws that already exist. California Penal Code 597 prohibits animal cruelty. The laws are written. The budgets are allocated. The salaries are paid. What is absent is the will to act.

We demand the following:

1. Real, sustained enforcement on Skid Row and across LA County, not press releases, not pilot programs that vanish after one action, but a permanent, properly resourced unit with the authority and mandate to investigate, seize animals in danger, and refer cases for prosecution. Results must be measurable and publicly reported.

2. Immediate reversal of proposed cuts to LA Animal Services and a commitment to increase, not reduce, staffing and resources at a time when shelters are overwhelmed, and animals across the county are dying from neglect.

3. Consistent prosecution under California Penal Code 597, regardless of ZIP code or housing status. Cruelty is cruelty. The law does not make exceptions, and neither should those paid to enforce it.

4. Transparent, publicly accessible reporting on animal cruelty complaints, response times, enforcement actions, and outcomes across the county, so that residents can hold you accountable for the results, not just the announcements.

5. Use of your public platforms to make unambiguously clear that animal cruelty in Los Angeles will not be tolerated, and that those responsible will face real legal consequences.

The suffering taking place on the streets of this city is not a secret. It is filmed daily, reported by credible news outlets, documented by rescue volunteers, and witnessed by thousands. The question is no longer whether it is happening. The question is why those being paid hundreds of thousands of dollars in public funds are allowing it to continue.

We are watching. We are organized. And we will not stop until the animals of Los Angeles County receive the protection they deserve are owed under the law.

Sincerely,



@HallieForAnimals Team
contact@hallieforanimals.org
links.callScript:
I’m calling to demand immediate action on the animal cruelty crisis in Los Angeles. Dogs and cats are suffering and dying on our streets, starved, beaten, and abandoned, while the agencies responsible for enforcing California Penal Code 597 fail to act.
This is unacceptable. My tax dollars fund your salaries, and I expect results.

links.mailtoBody:
To whom it may concern,

I am writing to report severe animal neglect and potential child welfare concerns at the property located at 356 E 79th St Los Angeles CA 90003.

Observed conditions:
- At least one severely matted dog is chained outside 24/7.
- Two additional dogs are chained at the back of the property.
- The property is in a filthy condition.
- The dogs water bowls are either empty or contain green water.
- The owner has been unresponsive to attempts by neighbors to speak with her and appears to talk to herself.

Toys visible in the windows suggest the possible presence of children in the home. Given the conditions of animal neglect and the owners apparent unresponsiveness, there are also serious concerns for the welfare of any children who may live there.

I respectfully request:
1. An immediate welfare check and investigation by LA Animal Services and/or LAPD Animal Cruelty Task Force for the chained dogs with removal/seizure if neglect or cruelty is confirmed.
2. A concurrent welfare check by DCFS for any children at the residence due to the unsafe and neglectful conditions.
3. Any necessary follow-up for mental health support or other county services if appropriate.

This situation has been ongoing and poses an immediate risk to the animals and any children present.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this urgent matter. 

Video evidence: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1GPcwpU9-2IELvoBqXQpV8Jtlgj7EbuY3/view?usp=sharing

Sincerely,



@HallieForAnimals Team
contact@hallieforanimals.org

The owner ignores anyone who tries to talk to her and talks to herself. Toys are visible in the windows, so children may be living there.

links.mailtoBody:
A quien corresponda:

Les escribo para denunciar formalmente un caso evidente de negligencia animal reiterada y posible abandono en el complejo de apartamentos Altos del Sendero 8, situado en la Avenida Las Palmeras 300, La Serena.

La situación afecta a un perro pequeño perteneciente al apartamento E304. Según varios vecinos, el animal es dejado completamente solo durante todo el día y toda la noche, de forma repetida y continuada, durante un período prolongado. Esto constituye un caso de negligencia continuada, que causa sufrimiento al animal y viola la Ley de Tenencia Responsable de Mascotas y Animales de Compañía de Chile (Ley 21.020).

Este asunto ya ha sido puesto en conocimiento de la administración del condominio y denunciado formalmente ante los Carabineros, pero hasta la fecha no se han implementado soluciones efectivas ni concretas. Los residentes están cada vez más preocupados por el bienestar del perro y solicitan la intervención inmediata del municipio.

Solicitamos que su Patrulla Municipal de Rescate de Animales tome las siguientes medidas urgentes:

- Realizar una inspección inmediata in situ del apartamento E304 para verificar las condiciones de vida del perro  
- Confirmar la identidad del propietario o propietarios responsables y evaluar si el animal está siendo cuidado adecuadamente  
- Garantizar que el perro reciba de inmediato los cuidados adecuados, incluyendo comida, agua, refugio y cualquier atención veterinaria necesaria  
- Aplicar todas las medidas coercitivas pertinentes previstas en la legislación chilena para evitar un mayor abandono o negligencia   

Gracias por su pronta atención a este urgente asunto de bienestar animal.

Prueba en vídeo: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1vYMVQWYtKyZLJnHHEJuqcant5RMOIKgm/view?usp=sharing

Atentamente,



Equipo de @HallieForAnimals
contact@hallieforanimals.org




To whom it may concern,

I am writing to formally report a clear case of repeated animal neglect and potential abandonment by Daniela Norambuena at the Altos del Sendero 8 condominium, Avenida Las Palmeras 300, La Serena.

The situation involves a small dog belonging to apartment E304. According to multiple neighbors, the animal is left completely alone for the entire day and throughout the night, on a repeated and ongoing basis, for a prolonged period. This constitutes ongoing neglect, causing distress to the animal and violating Chile’s Law on Responsible Ownership of Pets and Companion Animals (Law 21.020).

This matter has already been brought to the attention of the condominium administration and formally reported to Carabineros, yet no effective or concrete solutions have been implemented to date. The residents are increasingly worried about the dog’s welfare and are seeking immediate municipal intervention.

We request that your Municipal Animal Rescue Patrol take the following urgent actions:

- Conduct an immediate on-site inspection of the premises at apartment E304 to verify the living conditions of the dog  
- Confirm the identity of the responsible owner(s) and assess whether the animal is being properly cared for  
- Ensure the dog receives immediate proper care, including food, water, shelter, and any necessary veterinary attention  
- Apply all appropriate enforcement measures under Chilean law to prevent further neglect or abandonment   

Thank you for your prompt attention to this urgent animal welfare issue.

Video evidence: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1vYMVQWYtKyZLJnHHEJuqcant5RMOIKgm/view?usp=sharing

Sincerely,



@HallieForAnimals Team
contact@hallieforanimals.org

links.title: Arrest Rajaul Korim

links.mailtoBody:
যার জন্য প্রযোজ্য,

আমি জনাব এমডি রেজাউল করিম (যিনি রাজাউল করিম নামেও পরিচিত), ইনস্টাগ্রাম হ্যান্ডেল @rajaul88882024-এর বিরুদ্ধে বারবার পশু নির্যাতনের আনুষ্ঠানিক অভিযোগ দায়ের করতে লিখছি।

অবস্থান: পূর্বধলা বাজার, পূর্বধলা উপজেলা, নেত্রকোণা জেলা, ময়মনসিংহ বিভাগ, বাংলাদেশ।

বিবরণ:
উপরোক্ত অ্যাকাউন্টে ছোট ভিডিও রিল পোস্ট করা হয়, যেখানে রাস্তার কুকুরগুলোকে ইচ্ছাকৃতভাবে ভয় দেখানো হয়, তাড়া করা হয়, লাফিয়ে পড়া হয়, পায়ে ঝুলিয়ে দোলানো হয়, অথবা শুধুমাত্র বিনোদন ও ভাইরাল ভিউয়ের জন্য আতশবাজি-এর মতো বস্তু দিয়ে হয়রানি করা হয়। এতে প্রাণীদের স্পষ্ট কষ্ট, ভয় এবং সম্ভাব্য শারীরিক ক্ষতি হয়। এই ধরনের বিষয়বস্তু অব্যাহত রয়েছে।

এই আচরণ বাংলাদেশ পশু কল্যাণ আইন, ২০১৯ এবং দণ্ডবিধির পশুদের প্রতি নিষ্ঠুরতা সংক্রান্ত সাধারণ বিধিমালা লঙ্ঘন করে। আমি অনুরোধ করছি:

১. অবিলম্বে পুলিশের কাছে একটি সাধারণ ডায়েরি (জিডি)/এফআইআর রেজিস্টার করা হোক।  
২. অ্যাকাউন্টধারীকে শনাক্ত করে তার বিরুদ্ধে মামলা করা হোক।  
৩. ইনস্টাগ্রামের বিষয়বস্তু সরানো হোক এবং ভবিষ্যতের পোস্টগুলো মনিটর করা হোক।

এই বিষয়ে আপনার জরুরি মনোযোগের জন্য ধন্যবাদ।

ভিডিও প্রমাণ: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1Yx4pbvposY9yui-woVZs-sCv2364i78w/view?usp=sharing

বিনীত,



@HallieForAnimals টিম
contact@hallieforanimals.org




To whom it may concern,

I am writing to file a formal complaint of repeated animal cruelty against Mr. MD Rejaul Korim (also known as Rajaul Korim), Instagram handle @rajaul88882024.

Location: Purbadhala Bazar, Purbadhala Upazila, Netrokona District, Mymensingh Division, Bangladesh.

Details:  
The above account posts short video reels in which stray dogs are deliberately startled, chased, jumped on, swung around by the legs, or harassed with objects, such as firecrackers, purely for entertainment and viral views. This causes clear distress, fear, and potential physical harm to the animals. The content is ongoing.

This behaviour violates the Animal Welfare Act 2019 of Bangladesh as well as general provisions under the Penal Code regarding cruelty to animals. I request that:

1. A General Diary (GD)/FIR be registered immediately with Police.  
2. The account holder be identified and prosecuted.  
3. The Instagram content be removed and future posts monitored.

Thank you for your urgent attention to this matter.

Video evidence: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1Yx4pbvposY9yui-woVZs-sCv2364i78w/view?usp=sharing

Sincerely,



@HallieForAnimals Team
contact@hallieforanimals.org
links.callScript:
I'm calling to report a man who goes by Rajaul Korim in Purbadhala Bazar, Purbadhala Upazila, Netrokona.
He deliberately scares and harasses stray dogs by chasing, jumping on them, swinging them around by the legs, or harassing them with objects, such as firecrackers, for viral videos. This violates the Animal Welfare Act 2019.
Please register a GD or FIR and prosecute him. The more he scares these dogs, the more likely they are to start defending themselves.

links.title: Neglected Greyhounds near Parteen, Ireland
links.mailtoSubject: Greyhound Welfare Crisis Near Parteen, Co. Clare
links.description:
SEND THE ONE-CLICK EMAIL IN MY LINKTREE TO IRISH AUTHORITIES TITLED "NEGLECTED GREYHOUNDS NEAR PARTEEN, IRELAND"❗️

Call:
Cathal Crowe: +353 871 368 882
Timmy Dooley: +353 862 546 727
Joe Cooney: +353 872 500 227
Donna McGettigan: +353 617 031 08
DAFM Animal Welfare: +353 160 723 79
ISPCA: +353 433 325 035
Henry Street Garda Station: +353 612 124 00

Location: Near Parteen, Co. Clare, Ireland

No food, no water, one muzzled so couldn't drink even if it had water.
Welfare inspectors for Greyhound Racing Ireland (who have a financial interest) say they're fine.
DAFM and NCPCA won't get involved.
Cops are more interested in charging the whilsteblower for trespassing.
This is textbook neglect, but because these dogs generate revenue, no official body seems to care. If they were kids, the person who recorded would be hailed a hero.

#CountyClare #Parteen #animallover

links.mailtoBody:
To whom it may concern,

We're writing to formally raise the alarm regarding the welfare of greyhounds being kept in what can only be described as deplorable conditions at a location near Parteen, County Clare. Video evidence has been recorded documenting these conditions, including dogs without access to water and, critically, at least one dog wearing a muzzle, physically preventing it from drinking even if water were available. This isn't a matter of opinion, it's documented suffering.

What's deeply troubling isn't only the condition of these animals, but the institutional failure surrounding them:

— Two inspectors from Greyhound Racing Ireland visited the site and deemed conditions acceptable. This is an organisation with a direct financial interest in the greyhound racing industry. It should never have been in the position of conducting welfare checks in the first place. This is a textbook conflict of interest.

— DAFM and the NCPCA have declined to get involved.

— An Garda Síochána also deemed the conditions acceptable, and rather than investigate the welfare of suffering animals, they're instead pursuing a warrant to identify the individual who recorded the footage, with a view to charging them with trespassing.

If a child were found in equivalent conditions, no water, restrained from drinking, confined without adequate care, no authority in the civilized world would prosecute the person who filmed it. The act of exposure would be considered courageous, not criminal. These animals deserve no less consideration.

Ireland positions itself as a nation of animal lovers. And yet, it appears that as long as an animal generates revenue, its suffering is treated as acceptable collateral. Wales and Scotland have both moved to ban greyhound racing precisely because of the systemic neglect and abuse that accompanies the industry. Ireland must now ask itself what kind of nation it wishes to be.

We are calling on you to:

1. Ensure an independent welfare inspection is conducted immediately at the location near Parteen, Co. Clare, by a body with no financial ties to the greyhound racing industry.
2. Halt any legal proceedings against the individual who recorded the footage. Whistleblowers exposing animal cruelty should be protected, not prosecuted.
3. Launch an urgent review into the conflict of interest inherent in GRI conducting welfare inspections on racing greyhounds.
4. Consider legislative action in line with Wales and Scotland, where greyhound racing has been banned due to endemic welfare failures.

These animals cannot speak. We must.

We expect a public statement response, and we expect action.

Video evidence: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1T2tFLsdH4UoGtQCZrYrXBK9PFvRxawSp/view?usp=sharing

Sincerely,



@HallieForAnimals Team
contact@hallieforanimals.org
links.callScript:
I’m calling to report the neglect of greyhounds near Parteen, County Clare. Video evidence shows the dogs have no access to water and at least one is muzzled so it cannot drink.
We demand that these dogs be immediately removed from the neglectful situation and that the whistleblower who recorded the footage is not prosecuted.
Thank you.
